action: Add a two-copy external anchor strategy and adversarial tests for privileged deletion, filesystem replacement, and cross-device rename failure.

progress.finding: Two replicas improve one-copy recovery but are not a quorum. Accept matching copies, expose a single survivor only as degraded with mandatory repair, and reject divergent copies without an external trusted anchor.
progress.verification: 7 deterministic dependency-free Node tests passed in an isolated runtime against byte-identical source and test contents.

handoff: R-016 should define an external authority resolver for divergent replicas, using a separately administered signed or hardware-backed anchor, and prove that neither local replica can unilaterally override the retained external sequence or digest.
limits: No physical cross-device filesystem or power-loss test was performed.
Two local paths may share one compromise and failure domain.
Deletion or replacement of both replicas remains outside the trust boundary.
Replica agreement proves byte continuity only, not witness honesty or policy truth.
